title: PerturbSpec — declared perturbation neighborhood for ROBUST_NEIGHBORHOOD
description: ROBUST_NEIGHBORHOOD requires a DECLARED neighborhood, not an unstated intuition of 'similar cases'. The neighborhood is given either by a finite ENUMERATION of members or by a declared probability MEASURE — exactly one of the two (XOR). Invariants are what must NOT change; varied_fields are what does. The two sets are disjoint by construction: a field that is both invariant and varied makes the neighborhood incoherent, and the cross-record layer rejects it. metaorthemma_rebinding_rule states what happens to case-bound tokens as the case is perturbed — without it, robustness silently smuggles in a rebinding.

oneOf.description: declared probability measure over the neighborhood

neighborhood_family.description: the family of cases the neighborhood is drawn from

enumeration.description: finite list of neighborhood members (XOR with measure)

measure.description: declared probability measure over the neighborhood (XOR with enumeration)

invariants.description: fields held FIXED across the neighborhood

varied_fields.description: fields VARIED across the neighborhood; must be disjoint from invariants (cross-record check)

metaorthemma_rebinding_rule.description: what happens to case-bound tokens as the case varies: rebound, held, or the neighborhood is restricted to cases where the binding still holds

failure_criterion.description: what counts as a robustness failure over this neighborhood
